name: JCPOA Nuclear Deal
description: The Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action (JCPOA) was finalised in Vienna on 14 July 2015 between Iran and the P5+1 (US, UK, France, Russia, China, Germany) plus the EU. After 20 months of negotiations, Iran agreed to limit uranium enrichment to 3.67%, reduce its centrifuge count to 5,060 at Natanz, repurpose Fordow for non-enrichment research, and redesign the Arak heavy water reactor to eliminate plutonium production — all in exchange for lifting of UN, EU, and US nuclear-related sanctions. [Mohammad Javad Zarif](/people/javad-zarif) led Iran's negotiating team alongside [Abbas Araghchi](/people/abbas-araghchi) and [Ali Akbar Salehi](/people/ali-akbar-salehi). [Hassan Rouhani](/people/hassan-rouhani) championed the deal domestically against fierce hardliner opposition led by [Saeed Jalili](/people/saeed-jalili) and [Mahmoud Nabavian](/people/mahmoud-nabavian). The deal went into effect on 16 January 2016 ('Implementation Day') after IAEA verification. It was publicly opposed by Israel and Saudi Arabia.
editorial_notes: Finalised 14 July 2015. Adopted 18 October 2015. Implementation Day 16 January 2016. UN Security Council endorsed via Resolution 2231 (2015).
